title: Navy eyes Cambodian ships
desc: The navy is monitoring the activities of Cambodian fishing vessels after more than 20 trawlers encroached on Thai waters amid concerns they could be used for military purposes.

title: SMEs’ loan access to improve
desc: The government has launched a scheme to help small and medium-sized businesses access financing for commercial pickup trucks, targeting transport operators, farmers and freelancers.

title: Govt ‘ready’ for health summit
desc: The Ministry of Public Health says it is ready to host Global Wellness Summit 2026, noting that it aims to showcase Thai massage and herbal medicine on the global stage.

title: Army denies blocking return of displaced
desc: The Royal Thai Army (RTA) rejects allegations from Phnom Penh that Thai forces are blocking the return of tens of thousands of displaced people.

title: Govt moving to secure new energy supply sources
desc: The government is stepping up efforts to protect Thai nationals in conflict zones, including evacuation operations in Iran. It is also moving to secure alternative energy supplies and strengthen regional cooperation on energy and food security.

title: Fishermen seek relief for rising costs
desc: The National Fisheries Association of Thailand will hold a general meeting on Monday to discuss rising fuel costs and seafood prices and propose relief measures for the industry.

title: Fertiliser cargo stuck in strait
desc: Five fertiliser shipments remain stranded in the Strait of Hormuz, heightening supply concerns and prompting contingency measures, while Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul has ordered an urgent review of oil cost structures and the rollout of nationwide relief measures starting Wednesday.

title: Visas now in limbo for long-stay expats
desc: For Cui Heng, Thailand was never meant to be a loophole -- just a place to live.

title: Govt's visa-free policy 'too easy'
desc: The 60-day visa-free entry policy, which was intended to boost tourism recovery, is now under review as authorities weigh its economic benefits against security concerns.

title: Dead striped dolphin washes up on beach
desc: A striped dolphin was found dead on Bang Sak beach in Phangnga's Takua Pa district, the Department of Marine and Coastal Resources (DMCR) said.

title: Covid-19 Cicada variant poses low risk
desc: The Covid-19 variant Cicada, detected in the United States and 22 other countries, will eventually reach Thailand but poses no serious threat and requires no new vaccines, the Ministry of Public Health said.

title: Anutin apologises for fuel mismanagement
desc: Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul has apologised for fuel mismanagement in Thailand, saying the disruptions caused by the Middle East war have lasted longer than expected.

title: Anutin: New government to be in place next week
desc: Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul has confirmed that the list of cabinet ministers will be submitted for royal endorsement on Monday, saying all qualification issues have been resolved and a new government will be in place next week.

title: Why should anyone believe Anutin on fuel?
desc: After weeks of assurances that the nation’s fuel situation was “under control”, the Thai public was hit by a staggering 6-baht hike to fuel prices this week.

title: Man found dead in Udon Thani field after headshot
desc: Man found dead in Udon Thani field after headshot Legacy A man was found dead from a gunshot wound to the head in a field in Ban Dung district, Udon Thani , on March 27. His wife disclosed his alleged involvement with drug dealers, while police are also investigating a land sale dispute and a missing relative as part of the case.

title: Is the war a threat to subsea cable systems?
desc: State authorities and telecom operators are alert to potential risks to undersea cable systems stemming from the conflict in the Middle East, aiming to ensure their international connections in Thailand remain stable and unaffected.

title: Teen’s tragic death sparks call for school safety investigation
desc: Teen's tragic death sparks call for school safety investigation Legacy A 14 year old girl died following a motorcycle accident after her school allowed her to leave campus without notifying her guardian, raising concerns about student safety protocols and prompting calls for a formal investigation...

title: 阿努廷公开道歉：油价管控引波动，政府承认判断失误
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 3月28日，泰国总理兼内政部长阿努廷在媒体见面会上就近期油价管理引发的波动向公众致歉。他坦言，政府最初判断中东局势不会持续太久，但目前局势已发生变化，冲突可能长期化，因此政府必须不断调整政策措施...

title: Fake dollar bills distributed in Pattaya by tourists
desc: Fake dollar bills distributed in Pattaya by tourists Legacy Foreign tourists in Pattaya were caught distributing counterfeit US$100 bills while filming content on Thursday, March 27, prompting a public warning after some vendors nearly faced legal trouble...

title: Oil company accused of inflating fuel prices by 10 baht per litre
desc: Oil company accused of inflating fuel prices by 10 baht per litre Legacy An Ang Thong oil company has been reported to police for allegedly overcharging on fuel, adding nearly 10 baht per litre above the permitted transport cost...

title: 最新通报：霍尔木兹海峡遇袭货船3名失联船员仍未找到
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 据泰媒报道，3月28日上午，泰国外交部长西哈萨在政府大楼接受采访时表示，此前在霍尔木兹海峡附近海域遭遇意外的泰国散货船“Mayuree Naree”中，仍有3名失联船员尚未找到...

title: 曼谷骄傲节倒计时开启，300米彩虹旗创历史之最
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 3月28日，泰国“2026曼谷骄傲节”进入倒计时。据悉，今年将以“骄傲编织世界”为主题，并启用新的骄傲游行路线，全长约4.8公里...

title: 泰国成立专项工作组，管理霍尔木兹海峡本国通行船只
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 3月27日16点30分，泰国副总理兼交通部部长皮帕作为中东冲突局势管理中心负责人，委派能源部部长阿塔蓬主持召开会议，批准成立子工作组...

title: Bangkok Airways hikes ticket prices amid rising oil costs
desc: Bangkok Airways hikes ticket prices and adding fuel surcharges from April 1 as oil prices climb. CEO Puttipong Prasarttong-Osoth said domestic fares will rise by 15 to 20%...
